Stored Energy Resource.definition: A device which replenishes its Energy source (water, compressed air, battery, or flywheel) by withdrawing Energy from the system.

Substation.definition: The facility equipment that switches, changes or regulates electric voltage.

Supplemental Reserve.definition: Generation not connected to the system but capable of serving demand within a specified time or interruptible load that can be removed from the system in a specified time.

Supplemental Reserve Offer.definition: The price at which a supplemental-qualified resource has agreed to sell Supplemental Reserve in dollars per MW.

Supplemental-Qualified Resource.definition: A resource that has met the eligibility requirements to submit Supplemental Reserve Offers into the Energy and Operating Reserve Markets.

Supply Curve.definition: The aggregation of all Transmission Customer offer curves in the SPP footprint.

Switching Station.definition: The facility equipment used to tie together two or more electric circuits through switches. The switches are selectively arranged to permit a circuit to be disconnected or to change the electric connection between the circuits.

Sync-to-Min Time.definition: The time for a resource’s output to reach Minimum Economic Capacity Operating Limit following synchronization to the grid

Synchronized Resource.definition: A resource that is electrically connected to the grid by closing its circuit breaker.

System (Electric).definition: The physically connected generation, transmission, and distribution facilities operated as an integrated unit under one central management or operating supervision.

TC.definition: An eligible customer, or its designated agent, that can or does execute a transmission service agreement or can or does receive transmission service.

TCR.definition: A financial right entitling the holder to a share of the congestion revenue collected in the Day-Ahead Market.

TCR Funding.definition: A credit or a charge to an Asset Owner, calculated for each Transmission Congestion Right (TCR) instrument held by the owner. TCR instruments are fully funded each hour.

TCR Markets.definition: The Auction Revenue Rights' (ARRs’) Annual and Monthly Allocation Processes and the Annual and Monthly Transmission Congestion Right (TCR) Auctions.

TLR.definition: A procedure used to curtail Energy schedules to limit power flow across a transmission system element in order to avoid exceeding the equipment’s peak operating limits.

TMS.definition: Definition in North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC) of an infrastructure to consolidate data sharing (no present progress).

TO.definition: The North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC) functional entity that owns and maintains transmission facilities.

TP.definition: The North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC) functional entity that administers the transmission tariff and provides Transmission Service to Transmission Customers under applicable transmission service agreements.

TRM.definition: Amount of transmission transfer capability necessary to ensure interconnected transmission network is secure under a reasonable range of uncertainties in system conditions.

TSR.definition: Submitted through the SPP Open Access Same-Time Information System (OASIS) so eligible Transmission Customers can request transmission service.

Tariff.definition: Establishes services to be provided and rights and obligations of the parties pursuant to those services. It specifies rates, terms, and conditions under which Transmission Customers and Market Participants participate in the Integrated Marketplace.

Telemetry.definition: Electronic communication of the measurements and states related to the electric system’s elements. Telemetered information forms the basis for calculating settlement charge types, as well as determining the flows across transmission facilities and the status of circuit breakers and other devices.

Through Interchange Transaction.definition: A Market Participant schedule submitted between two external interfaces for use in the Day-Ahead Market or Real-Time Balancing Market for moving Energy through the SPP Balancing Authority Area.

Trading Hub.definition: A Settlement Location consisting of an aggregation of Price Nodes developed for financial and trading purposes. See Hub, Market Hub, or Resource Hub.

Transaction.definition: An agreement to transfer Energy from a seller to a buyer.

Transition State Offer.definition: An offer associated with a specific combined-cycle resource configuration, representing the cost of moving from the current configuration to another valid configuration.

Transition State Time.definition: An operating parameter associated with a specific combined-cycle resource configuration, representing the time required to move from the current configuration to another valid configuration.

Transmission.definition: The movement or transfer of electric energy over an interconnected group of lines and associated equipment between points of supply and points at which it is transformed for delivery to consumers or is delivered to other electric systems. Transmission is considered to end when the energy is transformed for distribution to the consumer.

Transmission Service.definition: The right to use high-voltage transmission lines to transfer power form a generator to a consumer.

Transmission System (Electric).definition: An interconnected group of electric transmission lines and associated equipment for moving or transferring electric energy in bulk between points of supply and points at which it is transformed for delivery over the distribution system lines to consumers or is delivered to other electric systems.

Transmitting Utility.definition: A regulated entity which owns, and may construct and maintain, wires used to transmit wholesale power. It may or may not handle the power dispatch and coordination functions. It is regulated to provide non-discriminatory connections, comparable service and cost recovery. According to the Energy Policy Act of 1992 (EPACT), this includes any electric utility, qualifying co-generation facility, qualifying small power production facility or Federal power marketing agency which owns or operates electric power transmission facilities which are used for the sale of electric energy at wholesale.

Turn-Around Ramp Rate Factor.definition: A percentage factor between 0-100% applied to a Resources Ramp-Rate-Up or Ramp-Rate-Down. The factor applies only in the next dispatch interval when the resource is issued a Dispatch Instruction that is in the opposite direction of the previous Dispatch Instruction.

UFE.definition: Calibration difference due to profile error, difference between actual losses and tariffed losses, theft, metering error.

UFLS.definition: Practice that uses relays to shed load to prevent transmission system collapse when frequency is declining.

URD.definition: The average MW amount of actual resource output in a dispatch interval above or below the resource’s average Setpoint Instruction in the dispatch interval.

Unbundling.definition: The separating of the total process of electric power service from generation to metering into its component parts for the purpose of separate pricing or service offerings.

Unused Regulation-Down Mileage.definition: The amount by which settled Regulation-Down mileage is less than the amount of Expected Regulation-Down Mileage.

Unused Regulation-Up Mileage.definition: The amount by which settled Regulation-Up mileage is less than the amount of Expected Regulation-Up Mileage.

VER.definition: A device for the production of electricity that is characterized by an energy source that: (1) is renewable; (2) cannot be stored by the facility owner or operator; and (3) has variability that is beyond the control of the facility owner or operator.

Virtual Energy Bid.definition: A Market Participant proposal to purchase Energy at a specified price, Settlement Location, and period of time in the Day-Ahead Market that is not associated with a physical load.

Virtual Energy Offer.definition: A Market Participant proposal to sell Energy at a specified price, Settlement Location, and period of time in the Day-Ahead Market that is not associated with a physical resource

Web Services.definition: A method to execute remote procedure calls via Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP).

Wholesale Competition.definition: A system whereby a distributor of power would have the option to buy its power from a variety of power producers, and the power producers would be able to compete to sell their power to a variety of distribution companies.

Wholesale Power Market.definition: The purchase and sale of electricity from generators to resellers (who sell to retail customers), along with the ancillary services needed to maintain reliability and power quality at the transmission level.

Wholesale Sales.definition: Considered the energy supplied to other electric utilities, cooperatives, municipals, and Federal and State electric agencies for resale to ultimate consumers.

Wholesale Transmission Services.definition: The transmission of electric energy sold, or to be sold, at wholesale in interstate commerce.

Withdrawal.definition: The removal of energy from the grid; the MW quantity is denoted as positive (+) in the imbalance energy equation.

XFR.definition: An electrical device for changing the voltage of Alternating Current (AC).

XML.definition: A flexible way to create standard information formats and share both the format and the data on the World Wide Web.

XSD.definition: Ensures that the information in your XML document is in a correct and consistent format and that all the necessary information has been included.

Zone.definition: The geographic area of the facilities of an SPP Transmission Owner (TO) or a specific combination of the TO as specified in the SPP Regional Tariff.

kV.definition: Measure of voltage for transmission, thousands of volts.

pu.definition: Per-unit [quantity per unit = the actual quantity /base quantity].

children.heading: Commercial Model & Node Hierarchy
children.purpose: How SPP maps physical buses to pricing and settlement points

items.label: Electrical Bus
items.members: Physical point on the transmission network (from the Network Operations Model); represents actual switches, breakers, and bus segments

items.label: Node
items.members: Logical representation of one or more electrical buses in the commercial model; the bridge between the physical network model and the market pricing model

items.label: Pricing Node (PNode)
items.members: A node where an LMP is calculated; each resource and load is associated with a PNode for dispatch and pricing

items.label: Aggregated Pricing Node (APNode)
items.members: A load-weighted average of multiple PNodes; used for load settlement locations to compute a single price for an area's load

items.label: Settlement Location
items.members: The financially meaningful point used for market settlement; can be a Resource, Load (APNode), Hub, or External Interface settlement location

children.heading: Reserve Zone
children.purpose: Predefined geographic areas for operating reserve and flexibility product allocation; studied twice per year to identify transmission-constrained areas
